title: Minimal Agent Loop
text: A minimal agent loop receives a task, builds context, decides whether a tool is needed, executes the allowed action, observes the result, and produces a bounded answer.

title: Tool Approval Policy
text: High-risk tool calls should require human approval before execution. Approval requests should include the tool name, arguments, reason, risk level, expected effect, and rollback plan.

title: Memory Policy
text: Agent memory should have explicit write, retrieve, update, delete, and audit rules. Sensitive data should not be stored by default.

title: RAG Evaluation
text: RAG evaluation should measure retrieval quality separately from answer quality. A good eval set includes direct lookup, synthesis, no-answer, and adversarial questions.

title: Production Safety
text: Production agents need observability, regression tests, cost limits, prompt-injection defenses, and human approval gates for high-impact actions.
